steps.title: Translate the first condition
steps.content: "Lena has 3 times as many dollars as Omar" means Lena's amount equals 3 times Omar's: $l = 3o$.

steps.title: Translate the second condition
steps.content: "Together they have a total of \$36.00" means $l + o = 36$.

steps.title: Eliminate choices
steps.content:
• "$l=3o$ $l+o=36$": Matches both translations exactly. ✓
  • "$o=3l$ $l+o=36$": $o=3l$ would mean Omar has 3 times Lena's amount, reversing the relationship. ✗
  • "$l=36o$ $l+o=3$": Swaps the roles of 3 and 36; total would be 3 dollars, not 36 dollars. ✗
  • "$o=36l$ $l+o=3$": Both equations are wrong — wrong multiplier and wrong total. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
